@graph.name: Mercedes-Benz classic models fitted with the OM621/OM622/OM626 family

mainEntity.name: How much does an OM621/OM622/OM626 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned OM-series diesel typically costs £1,200–£2,500 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by displacement and variant. Fitting adds £300–£600 depending on labour rates and vehicle model.

mainEntity.name: Is the OM621/OM622/OM626 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — these engines rate 4.5★ out of 5 based on decades of owner data [MB-INTERVIEW]. They are legendary for their extreme longevity, with many examples exceeding 300,000 miles when maintained.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common OM621/OM622/OM626 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Injection pump wear is the most common issue, causing starting problems. Glow plug failure and fuel system air leaks are also frequent on older examples.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes models use the OM621/OM622/OM626?

acceptedAnswer.text: These engines power the W110 (190D), W114/W115 (Stroke 8, 200D/220D/240D), W123 (200D/220D/240D), and W201 (190D, 190D 2.5) — classic Mercedes from 1958 to 1988.

mainEntity.name: How long does the OM621/OM622/OM626 last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With simple maintenance, these engines routinely exceed 300,000 miles. Many specialists report examples at 500,000+ with original internals — one of the most durable engines ever built [MB-INTERVIEW].

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz OM621/OM622/OM626 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Mercedes OM621/OM622/OM626 are legendary pre-chamber diesels — known for extreme longevity and simplicity. Backed by UK specialist consensus and classic car community insight [MB-INTERVIEW].

verdictRating.confidence: 90% confidence — based on decades of owner data and specialist knowledge of these classic diesel engines.

rows.score: 20 (Most failures are cheap, well‑understood repairs (injector pumps, glow plugs))
16 (Good availability through classic specialists, but some parts are becoming scarce)
20 (Reconditioned cost well under 30% of classic vehicle values)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The OM621/OM622/OM626 are legendary for their endurance and simplicity — injection pump and glow plug issues are common but easy to resolve — buy on rust-free shell, not engine condition.
verdictRating.bestFor: Classic Mercedes enthusiasts wanting bulletproof mechanical diesels.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You want modern power, refinement, or a fast diesel.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an OM621/OM622/OM626 typically takes 8–12 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].
